headline: Plantation Workers' Wages Still Below Living Wage at RM1,800
context: The Malaysian Estate Owners Association reported that average plantation worker wages reached RM1,800 monthly in 2025, still 28% below Bank Negara's estimated living wage of RM2,500 for a single adult in rural areas.

cards.big: RM1,800 monthly for estate workers — 28% below living wage while plantation companies earned RM14 billion in 2025 profits
cards.sub: Top 5 plantation GLCs paid RM2.8 billion in dividends but their workers can't afford basic living costs

cards.big: Plantation sector employs 440,000 workers — 82% foreign nationals who accept wages below domestic workers' expectations
cards.sub: Labour cost is only 22% of plantation revenue — raising wages to RM2,500 would reduce profit margins by just 4.2%

cards.big: Estate housing for workers: 68% of quarters built before 1990 with documented overcrowding and sanitation issues
cards.sub: Workers' children attend estate schools with 30% lower SPM pass rates than national average

cards.big: EU deforestation regulation requires labour rights compliance — but Malaysian plantation audit standards fall short of ILO conventions
cards.sub: MSPO sustainability certification covers environmental standards but has weak enforcement of worker welfare provisions

cards.big: An industry earning RM14 billion while its workers live below poverty line isn't a wage problem — it's a values problem
Should plantation companies be required to pay living wages as a condition of MSPO certification and export access?
